segments.text: My brethren,
do not hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory, with partiality.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For if there should come into your assembly a man with gold rings, in fine apparel, and there should also come in a poor man in filthy clothes,
and you pay attention to the one wearing the fine clothes and say to him,
You sit here in a good place,
segments.speaker: Unnamed Person

segments.text: and say to the poor man,
You stand there, or, Sit here at my footstool,
segments.speaker: Unnamed Person

segments.text: have you not shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Listen, my beloved brethren:
Has God not chosen the poor of this world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom which He promised to those who love Him?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: But you have dishonored the poor man. Do not the rich oppress you and drag you into the courts?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Do they not blaspheme that noble name by which you are called?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: If you really fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself,' you do well;
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: but if you show partiality, you commit sin, and are convicted by the law as transgressors.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For whoever shall keep the whole law, and yet stumble in one point, he is guilty of all.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For He who said, 'Do not commit adultery,' also said, 'Do not murder.' Now if you do not commit adultery, but you do murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: So speak and so do as those who will be judged by the law of liberty.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For judgment is without mercy to the one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: What does it profit, my brethren, if someone says he has faith but does not have works? Can faith save him?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: If a brother or sister is naked and destitute of daily food,
and one of you says to them,
Depart in peace, be warmed and filled,
segments.speaker: Unnamed Person

segments.text: but you do not give them the things which are needed for the body, what does it profit?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Thus also faith by itself, if it does not have works, is dead.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: But someone will say,
You have faith, and I have works.
segments.speaker: Unnamed Person

segments.text: Show me your faith without your works, and I will show you my faith by my works.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: You believe that there is one God. You do well. Even the demons believe—and tremble!
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: But do you want to know, O foolish man, that faith without works is dead?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered Isaac his son on the altar?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Do you see that faith was working together with his works, and by works faith was made perfect?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, 'Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.' And he was called the friend of God.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: You see then that a man is justified by works, and not by faith only.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Likewise, was not Rahab the harlot also justified by works when she received the messengers and sent them out another way?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.
segments.speaker: James
